desc: Cartagena combines a walled colonial old town, Caribbean heat, and nearby island beaches, working well for travelers who want culture, food, and sea time in one base.
hls: The Walled City and Getsemaní are the core zones for architecture, nightlife, and restaurant density.
Driest, most comfortable period is usually January-March, but it is also peak demand season.
Rain and humidity climb from roughly May to November, with October often the wettest month.
Rosario Islands day trips are popular year-round but sea conditions vary by wind and season.
A 3-5 night stay balances old-town exploration, food, and one beach/island day.
Shoulder periods (April, November) can reduce price pressure while still keeping itineraries workable.

todo.name: Walled City heritage walk
todo.desc: Do an early or sunset walking circuit through plazas, churches, and defensive walls before midday heat peaks.

todo.name: Getsemaní street-art and café route
todo.desc: Explore murals and backstreets, then anchor at Plaza de la Trinidad for evening atmosphere.

todo.name: Castillo San Felipe visit
todo.desc: Tour the hilltop fortress and tunnels for context on Cartagena’s colonial defense history.

todo.name: Rosario Islands boat day
todo.desc: Book a reputable operator for snorkeling or beach stops, and choose slower itineraries if you dislike crowded party boats.

todo.name: Bazurto or guided market food tour
todo.desc: Sample Caribbean coastal dishes with a guide if you want deeper local cuisine beyond old-town menus.

todo.name: Sunset wall or bay plan
todo.desc: Reserve one evening for sunset on the city walls or a short bay cruise, then dinner in Centro/Getsemaní.

wifi.note: Strong in established hotels and cowork-friendly cafés; expect variability in older buildings and island excursions.

prac.fltNote: No routine UK nonstop service; most itineraries connect via Madrid, Bogotá, Panama City, or U.S./LATAM hubs. Fares typically spike for Christmas/New Year and July-August travel windows.
prac.currency: Colombian Peso (COP). Cards are widely accepted in tourist districts, but cash helps for small vendors and some taxis.
prac.lang: Spanish is primary; English is common in tourism-facing hotels/restaurants but less consistent beyond core districts.
prac.visa: Colombia entry and onward-travel requirements vary by passport; check official immigration rules and stay limits before departure.
bestFor: Colonial city breaks with beach add-ons
alerts: Heat index and humidity are high year-round—plan indoor breaks and hydrate aggressively.
Use licensed taxis or app rides at night, especially outside the old-town core.
For island trips, confirm boat safety standards, return times, and weather cancellation terms.

sweet: January to March is usually the top window for driest weather and smoother sightseeing, while April and November often offer better value with manageable trade-offs.
